cap-setup.title: Set Up Your Team's Shared AI Infrastructure
cap-setup.taskPrompt: Your team (a handful of devs plus some non-dev knowledge workers, on a mix of Windows/Mac/Linux, light governance, on the Team plan) is adopting Claude Code and Codex. Lay out the shared setup: which surfaces for whom, what config lives where (the four tiers), how it spans Claude and Codex, and which non-negotiables you enforce.
cap-setup.openEndedPrompt: Write your team's setup decision: surfaces by audience, what lives in each of the four tiers, how Claude and Codex stay aligned, and the two or three things you enforce. End with a one-paragraph rationale.

criteria.description: Routes each audience/task to a fitting surface (CLI / Desktop / Cowork / Codex) and notes what each can't do.
Places each shared thing in the right tier: repo .claude, marketplace plugin, managed settings, or personal.
Spans Claude + Codex deliberately (AGENTS.md / portable skills), not by assuming inheritance.
Enforces the non-negotiables via managed settings; keeps secrets out; right-sizes governance to the team.
States the decision and a short, revisitable rationale rather than listing features.

fatalMisconceptions.description: Relies on a worded CLAUDE.md to enforce a security must-not.
Assumes Codex inherits CLAUDE.md / Claude's skills automatically.
Puts a secret in committed config because the repo is private.
Treats the surfaces as rivals and standardizes on exactly one.
Puts all shared config in one place instead of the right tier.
